freshness_policy: Use exact completed 2026 Wikimedia dump snapshots. Do not follow moving latest aliases after acquisition. Do not silently fall back to an older snapshot.

gptnl_policy.recommendation: Replace GPT-NL in Stage A v1 with the three official Wikimedia dump candidates below. Keep GPT-NL blocked. Reconsider GPT-NL only in a later versioned packet after every selected shard reports security status safe and an ablation shows unique value.

sources.name: Simple English Wikipedia
sources.license: Wikimedia textual content: CC BY-SA 4.0 and GFDL where applicable; retain dump-specific attribution and notices.
sources.acquisition_method: Download the exact completed XML dump and index, verify the archive against the official SHA-1 list, calculate a local SHA-256, and lock all URLs, sizes, hashes, and acquisition time.
quality_filters: main namespace only
reject redirects, disambiguation-only pages, lists, tables, infobox-only pages, stubs, and template-dominated text
remove references, navigation, categories, edit metadata, markup residue, and repeated boilerplate
require coherent paragraphs and useful sentence density
reject personal data, secret-shaped values, prompt injection text, vandalism indicators, and malformed Unicode
cap biography, geography, date, and entertainment concentration
prefer explanatory pages with clear subject-verb structure
dedup_and_leakage: comparison against all Stage A repository candidates
comparison against every field in all evaluation suites
risks: simplified prose can be repetitive or child-oriented
community pages may contain vandalism or weak facts
encyclopedic style does not teach developer dialogue
sources.why_more_controllable_than_gptnl: One official text-only XML snapshot with public checksums and page-level revision metadata can be parsed without executing files or deserializing remote parquet shards.

sources.name: English Wikibooks
sources.license: Wikimedia textual content: CC BY-SA 4.0 and GFDL where applicable; retain dump-specific attribution and notices.
sources.acquisition_method: Download the exact completed XML dump and index, verify the official SHA-1, calculate a local SHA-256, and retain page/revision provenance.
quality_filters: main namespace only
retain complete tutorial sections and explanatory paragraphs
prefer computing, mathematics, science, writing, and practical problem-solving books
reject abandoned outlines, empty chapters, answer keys without questions, navigation pages, recipes dominated by lists, and copied manuals
reject obsolete technical instructions unless explicitly labeled historical
remove markup, references, categories, and repeated book navigation
cap any single book family to prevent style domination
dedup_and_leakage: cross-source comparison with official project documentation
comparison against instructions and all evaluation fields
risks: some books are unfinished or obsolete
multiple chapters may repeat introductions and navigation
sources.why_more_controllable_than_gptnl: The dump is a bounded official archive with explicit page hierarchy, stable identifiers, public checksums, and a single known licensing regime.

sources.name: English Wikiversity
sources.license: Wikimedia textual content: CC BY-SA 4.0 and GFDL where applicable; retain dump-specific attribution and notices.
sources.acquisition_method: Download the exact completed XML dump and index, verify the official SHA-1, calculate a local SHA-256, and retain page/revision provenance.
quality_filters: main namespace only
prefer coherent lessons, worked explanations, definitions, and problem-solving discussions
reject course administration, enrollment, discussion, user, quiz-answer-only, and navigation pages
reject speculative research notes presented as fact
remove markup, references, categories, repeated headers, and templates
require minimum paragraph and sentence quality
cap subject and course-family concentration
dedup_and_leakage: cross-source comparison with Wikibooks and repository documentation
comparison against instructions and every evaluation field
risks: open course pages have uneven review quality
administrative and discussion content can dominate
some lessons contain unsupported or unfinished claims
sources.why_more_controllable_than_gptnl: The official XML snapshot is bounded, checksum-verifiable, text-oriented, and provides stable page and revision identifiers for rejection and lineage tracking.
